headline: Evolving Narrative For Tesco (LSE:TSCO) As Analysts Weigh Upside Against Execution Risks
summary: Tesco’s assessed fair value is now set at £4.89, compared with £4.86 previously, while some individual price targets, such as JPMorgan’s move to 480 GBp from 500 GBp, point in a different direction. This mix of higher fair value estimates and trimmed targets reflects how analysts are weighing potential upside against execution and near term risks. headline: Why The Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM) Story Is Shifting With New Policy And Valuation Views
summary: Archer-Daniels-Midland’s fair value estimate has been updated to about US$63.82 from roughly US$62.09, a modest shift that puts fresh attention on how analysts are recalibrating expectations. Several firms are pairing this with higher price targets, such as moves to US$68 from US$61 and JPMorgan’s US$4 increase, as they weigh policy support, commodity trends, and a mixed sector backdrop. headline: Shares of bank companies are trading higher after President Trump's social media post indicated that the U.S. will work closely with Iran, and the countries have already agreed to several key points in the proposed conflict-resolution plan. The sector is also benefiting from easing energy prices and lower Treasury yields, which are helping to alleviate inflation concerns and support overall market sentiment.

headline: Jamie Dimon Basically Says: Some People In Private Credit Have No Business Being In Private Credit
summary: As risks quietly build across the $3 trillion private credit market, Jamie Dimon is warning that the next downturn could expose weaker lenders—though regulators say the fallout is unlikely to threaten the broader financial system. The JPMorgan CEO, in a letter to shareholders, wrote that "it's always been true that not everyone providing credit is necessarily good at it," when speaking about the current private credit environment. headline: Exxon Lost 6% of Its Global Output as War Upended Mideast
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Exxon Mobil Corp. lost 6% of its global production in the first quarter as the Iran war paralyzed oil and natural gas operations in the Persian Gulf.Half those outages were from a liquefied natural gas complex in Qatar in which Exxon is a partner, the company said Wednesday. Two LNG production lines, or trains, at the facility were damaged by Iranian missile strikes.“Public reports indicate the damage will take a prolonged period to repair,” Exxon said in a statement. “Pending an

headline: Global Bonds Jump as Ceasefire Sows Doubts Over Steep Rate Hikes
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Global bonds surged Wednesday on news that the US and Iran had agreed to a ceasefire, pausing a war that roiled markets for weeks by delivering the worst oil shock in years. European debt, at the heart of the selloff due to the region’s exposure to soaring energy prices, led the gains with some yields falling more than 25 basis points as traders slashed wagers on interest-rate hikes. As part of the two-week truce, Tehran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial transit rou

headline: Delta Air Lines Flags $2 Billion Iran Fuel Hit. Why the Stock Is Flying After Earnings.
summary: Delta Air Lines stock was surging ahead of the open Wednesday as the carrier’s earnings and the two-week U.S., Iran cease-fire deal proved to be tailwinds for the shares.  Demand is so strong that Delta expects to post a $1 billion pretax profit in the current quarter despite a more than $2 billion increase in fuel costs.  The carrier is also cutting capacity to mitigate the impact of higher fuel prices.
